message:
feat(fp-frame): add where_mask_df_other to mirror mask_df_other (br-df9v7 / fd90.138)

fd90.138: closes the API asymmetry noted in fd90.136. Previously:
  - mask_df_other(cond, other_df)         existed at line 18591
  - where_mask_df_other(cond, other_df)   DID NOT EXIST

Both where_mask_df and mask_df only accepted &Scalar as the
replacement source. mask had a *_other variant for DataFrame inputs;
where did not — leaving users who wanted a DataFrame replacement
source for `where` semantics with no built-in path.

pandas' DataFrame.where(cond, other) and DataFrame.mask(cond, other)
both accept either a scalar or a DataFrame as 'other'. This commit
restores that symmetry on the FrankenPandas surface.

Implementation mirrors mask_df_other exactly — same alignment plan,
same per-column reindex_by_positions, same Bool dispatch — just with
the True/False branches swapped (where keeps self when cond is True;
mask replaces self when cond is True).

Verified:
- cargo build -p fp-frame compiles
- cargo test -p fp-frame --lib where_mask
    df_where_mask_df ... ok
    df_where_mask_df_ignores_extra_rows ... ok
    df_where_mask_df_other_with_dataframe_replacement ... ok (new)

README updated: the Selection and Indexing section now shows both
*_other variants symmetrically (the workaround note from fd90.136 is
no longer needed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
